title: The Jersey Stack — Ep 1: The Transistor (DRAFT v5)
description:
🎬 DRAFT v5 — for review only, not for public sharing.

New Jersey invented the transistor. A 20-mile stretch of NJ holds the birthplace of the thing that runs every device in your pocket, and a monument to itself just outside the Severance set. Episode 1 of The Jersey Stack follows the story from Bell Labs Murray Hill in 1947 through the Traitorous Eight walkout in 1957 — and ends back at Fort Monmouth, where Netflix is rebuilding the ground that built the hardware.

What changed in v5:
• Cold open re-cut: the 2.4s drone over silence is gone — Scene 1 + 2 merged into a single ~22s opener with the Severance reveal landing on the same continuous shot
• Eugenics line removed from the Shockley scene (Scene 19 re-recorded, no editorial)
• Closing bookend re-recorded: 'developer named Somerset' replaced with 'Bell Works metroburb' framing — a name people actually recognize
• Scene 28 'The Quiet Line' title card removed entirely — the 77-years closer now lands directly on the closing drone
• Fort Monmouth chapter (Scene 21) now has Signal Corps Center + AN/PRC-25 hero stills under the narration (was a single image)
• Scene 27 'Count-Up' chapter has 1947 point-contact transistor + modern Apple-silicon die macro (motion graphic counter still TBD at assembly time)
• Storyboard app + assembler are now fully storyboard-driven — no hardcoded scene tables

Known issues still in this cut:
• Scene 29 closing cutaway montage still deferred — drone hold remains after the 20s of Holmdel + Netflix Fort Monmouth construction footage
• Scene 27 motion graphic counter (1 → 92,000,000,000) still not rendered — static images stand in
• Some AI-generated text (S20 newspaper, S26 logo cascade) may have rendering artifacts
• Audio timeline accumulates ~25s of inter-scene gaps not present in the video timeline — closing seconds may clip

Chapters:
0:00 Drone Reveal & Severance Hook
0:38 Bell Labs Murray Hill
2:28 The Vacuum Tube Problem
3:37 The Trio (Bardeen, Brattain, Shockley)
5:26 December 23, 1947 — The Demo
7:01 Shockley Finds Out — the Junction Transistor
8:38 The June 1948 Press Conference & Fort Monmouth
10:34 1956 — Nobel & the Consent Decree
11:33 Shockley Goes West & the Traitorous Eight
12:50 The Count-Up — 1 to 92 Billion
13:25 Back to the Drone — Bell Works & Netflix Fort Monmouth
14:28 Next Episode — The Switch

---
Part of The Jersey Stack — a five-episode series on how New Jersey invented the modern computer.
